It definitely is a quality controller and people shouldn’t sleep on it.

A store my buddy works at had a mega clearance sale for “old” iPads (M1) for more then 50% off and I showed up and got a 5th gen 12.9 inch iPad Pro for a little over $400 before tax.  iPad Air M1s were sub $300 during the sale.  Spec-wise, The m2 is a very incremental upgrade over the m1 and the m4 is only a little under a 50% improvement in power and you still get the 120 hz screen with this pro (also he told me that the same thing happens for Samsung tablets)

trigger_phrase: I have both the Xiaomi Pad 5 and 6
market_implication: Repeat purchase behavior across consecutive generations suggests strong product loyalty and customer satisfaction with Xiaomi's tablet offering.
text:
I have both the Xiaomi Pad 5 and 6 - both have very good screens, CPU and audio. I find that Chinese tablets tend to have the best bang for buck, but you tend to have to wait a bit for a global ROM version before they turn up in my country (the UK).

I know this was coming from a "normie" angle, but the main way to extend the update life of Android tablets is via custom ROMs. It would have been nice if the Labs had discovered if a) the bootloader was unlockable and b) if there were any custom ROMs for the tablet.

trigger_phrase: ended up being so useful for me
market_implication: Strong value perception and willingness to spend on accessories bundle indicates pricing power and accessory revenue opportunity for Lenovo tablets.
text: Lenovo Tab P12 for best large screen android tablet at around $300 (depending on accessories bundles and seem to be often on deep sales). I managed to snag one WITH the keyboard case and pen for only $350 (including taxes and shipping at the time). That same sale is running right now. It ended up being so useful for me as a deaf person since the mics on these was surprisingly a lot better than I expected, aiding the Google's Live Transcribe feature that allows me to stay involved in meetings and whatnot in real time :) One nice thing about this is that Best Buy also sells them so It was a great way for me to double check how I liked the form factor and the software before pulling the trigger (slowly losing vision so had to double check the accessibility features first). Ordered it online instead of via Best Buy for that accessories bundle sale.

trigger_phrase: Salon gel style nails CAN BE DONE AT HOME for a fraction of the cost
market_implication: Consumer awareness of DIY alternatives to professional salon services could pressure salon margins and shift demand toward retail beauty supply retailers.
text:
Just a note for my fellow nail-lovers on a budget out there:  Salon gel style nails CAN BE DONE AT HOME for a fraction of the cost.  The start up investment of an LED lamp, a base and top coat, a builder gel, a bottle of acetone, bag of cotton balls and aluminum foil, and a palette of 7 basic gel colors, can often be had for the cost of a single gel manicure at the salon.  YouTube tutorials are free.

A little bit of practice is involved, and doing it yourself takes twice as long since you can only do one hand at a time, but if you're in a position where you have more time than money, its totally worthwhile to learn. I have bling bling $100 value nails on for about two hours of my time every two weeks, and haven't spent more than about $50 total on my supplies in over a year.   And I use it as an opportunity for some self care time / me time.   (I still love the luxury of being able to go to the salon, but if you're running low on money and that's what you have to cut, you don't have to give up on that part of your beauty routine.)

trigger_phrase: i wear a simple fitbit because i love that it counts my steps but i've come to realise any other more complex wearable is simply not for me
market_implication: Consumer abandoning complex wearables like Whoop in favor of simpler Fitbit alternative, citing trust and placebo concerns.
text:
hey Laura another fantastic video and a truthful review so thank you. 

i wear a simple fitbit because i love that it counts my steps but i've come to realise any other more complex wearable is simply not for me. Everybody is different and I can see why whoop, apple watch, garmin etc have become so popular as more and more people want to be healthy, but personally i am massively susceptible to the placebo (and nocebo) effect.

no one knows my body better than me. problems ive had in the past with a wearable such as whoop are: ive slept well but when checking my wearable it says i have not. i then believe it over my own bodily and brain thoughts and i end up feeling tired the rest of the day even though there is plenty of data now showing the strength of thinking you have a good sleep actually makes you go through your day without feeling the effects one would if they were tired for example. 

Another one is when and when not to train. the whoop outsources the skill of being able to read and understand your body. one relies on a wearable to tell them if they feel recovered or not. As someone who trains a lot i now know very well the difference of when i feel 'lazy' and when i know i need to take a break as i feel fatigued and need time to recover by not working out that day. 
i sometimes think...how many more things are we going to outsource to wearables...will the whoop one day tell us: 
'you are only 43% full, aim to fill your stomach to 80% in a meal' 😅
there are some good data points like the HR variability, but some like the sleep analysis and recovery i just feel remove yet another thing we should be able to feel within our own bodies like the use of a calculator on our phone abolished the ability for many of the younger generation to do simple maths sums in their head.

another one was my menstrual cycle. theres been anecdotal data that strength can oscillate during the cycle eg strongest in follicular, weakest in luteal etc. as you know i train hard and have

trigger_phrase: I absolutely love using the Whoop. It has changed my life... total game changer
market_implication: Strong user satisfaction and behavioral lock-in indicating high retention and potential upsell/expansion revenue.
text: Despite the frustrations I mentioned in my other comment, I absolutely love using the Whoop. It has changed my life. Like you, I’ve found it very motivating when it comes to making positive changes for my health and well-being. What’s been a total game changer for me, is that it has helped me to pinpoint where I was going wrong. Sure, we can all guess that we should eat healthy, drink plenty of water, exercise regularly, try to sleep well etc…. But with the Whoop device, I have been able to see just how much it effects my well-being if I - for example - have a really stressful day, or a disrupted night of sleep, or if I forget to drink properly. Seeing the data there in black and white has really motivated me to make changes for the better. And alongside this, it’s indirectly helped me to stick to a super-nutritious diet (diet as in way of eating, I’m not trying to lose weight) as I’m so motivated to do what’s best for my health.

trigger_phrase: I find it fascinating when people get defensive (and even a little angry) about what other people spend their money on i.e. a Whoop
market_implication: User demonstrates willingness to defend premium pricing and spending on Whoop, suggesting strong product satisfaction despite cost concerns from critics.
text:
Notes about this video:
1. I've started wearing the Whoop censor on the inside of my wrist (see from 10:05 in the video). At moment, it seems to be making a difference and is more accurate for of the time. I also tightened it up a bit.

2. Some people in the comments confused to whether a Whoop is ideal for them.
If you really want to delve deep and make changes to your health with sleep patterns, increase exercise, increase certain training zones, get specific advice based on your specific data each day, you don't have data anxiety, this would be ideal for you.
If you're a massive runner, a Garmin would be much better, as you have a screen and stats you can see when you run.

3. I find it fascinating when people get defensive (and even a little angry) about what other people spend their money on i.e. a Whoop. 
Some people drive a Nissan, other people decide to buy a Mercedes, and some even pre extravagant.  
I mention this, because this is a little like that. Garmin gives certain things, Apple Watch something else, and a Whoop something slightly different again. Like cars, they all do a similar thing, but a slightly different way.
For those that get wound up, there's no need 😂

4. And to the person who said "Just don't get this video from you".... bear in mind, because I share some of my life and thoughts on the internet, and you watch my videos, doesn't mean you know me or could guess how I think or feel about certain things.
Just a polite reminder.

text: I absolutely love the Haruharu probiotic barrier essence for my extremely dry eczema rosacea menopausal skin - it's a must have and a genuine replacement for the previous incarnation of the Laneige cream skin (the new cerapep version is NOT a good replacement IMO 🤬). I'm on my fifth bottle but I agree oily skin will probably hate it😂 - they don't need more oil whereas dry skin does as dry means a lack of oil (dehydration can affect every skin type).  Best wishes x

trigger_phrase: I have gone through more than 7 bottles, I have gone through one bottle in 3 weeks
market_implication: Extremely high repeat purchase rate and consumption velocity suggest strong product-market fit and pricing power for skincare brand.
text: The Eqqualberry bakuchiol serum actually saved my skin so... I don´t think it is for sensitive skin but those bumps, I never had an issue that they would not melt in more than 10 seconds so it is weird to seeing you struggle with that🤔... I have gone trough more than 7 botles, I have combo skin and had the worst hyperpigmentation, nothing was helping and everything was breaking me out .. and I don´t have sensitive skin, just pore clogging... then one friend recomended me this serum and I have gone through one botle in 3 weeks and that hyperpigmentation which I have had for more than half year, it was deep, almost like purple color, it started faded😶‍🌫... I was using it every morning, just this and sunscreen... but of course each skin is diferent, I know you love VT pdrn essence and serum and for me? It clogged my pores so much overnight, like literally nothing ever broke me and gave me those big under skin bumps like this essence and serum... both are terrible! But I guess I should see it coming since second ingridient is Isopropyl Myristate and my dermatologist warned me, she said NEVER use products with this because it is very pore clogging... I just forgot to check, lesson learned I never skip chcecking ingridients list since this experience 😅🥲

text: I’m obsessed with the Eqqualberry Swimming Pool toner. It’s has a holygrail status for me. I use it every single day twice daily and I couldn’t get enough of it lol! It certainly made a big difference on my skin when it comes to texture, clarity and radiance! I couldn’t be without it lol

trigger_phrase: One of the best purchase of mine
market_implication: Customer satisfaction with warranty service and product quality indicates strong brand loyalty and repeat purchase intent despite minor usability complaints.
text:
Purchased last year after watching your video...
One of the best purchase of mine...
A few month back the water pump was not working but as its covered under 3 year warranty so it was replaced for free with a door step service by Bajaj. The only con which I found with this cooler is cleaning it It's not easy , even if after opening two sides of the cooler which is quite easy to open you can not put your hand or brush inside the water tank to wash  inside it.
Beside this as a personal cooler its the best cooler available.
